content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes t test cases as input, then for each test case, reads an integer N, and prints N + 1.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (‑1000000000 ≤ N ≤ 1000000000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with the value N+1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each N is increased by one: 4→5, 2→3, -1→0.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. Loop T times, each time read N, compute N+1 using 64‑bit integer arithmetic, and output the result. No preprocessing or complex data structures are required.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored regardless of T.
